description: Develop or continue in good health under the surrounding conditions. A child, plant, animal, or community can thrive. 'ruloi' (flourish) describes abundant growth or activity; 'welira' asks how the living subject fares in health.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then open into middle-height front 'e'. Set the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for stressed 'l' and release into high-front 'i'. Tap the ridge briefly for 'r' before final open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: Glide, lateral, and tap give the word a light forward rhythm without a full consonant closure after its opening. Its easy continuity recalls a life doing well in its conditions.
usage_notes: A thriving subject takes no direct object. A 'mua' phrase can name the surrounding conditions. 'wolu' (healthy) states present health; 'welira' follows how health and development fare through time.

examples.translation: The child thrives in a good home.

pillars.solarpunk-values: The Solarpunk Manifesto uses 'welira' for life without energy stored from ancient life. The verb puts the claim on the health of lives and communities, where its consequences can be examined.

semantic_domains.community: a community developing in sustained good health
semantic_domains.nature: healthy development in an organism or population
semantic_domains.physical: a living body's health across development
semantic_domains.temporal: good health and development continuing through time
